First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Discuss Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church using “Words to Know”and the discussion questions in Chapter 21 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). The exercisescould be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions in Chapter 21 ofthe activity book as a guide.) These can be done orally. The discussion will prepare you for the summaries in the next two days.Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 2Lesson 8, Selection 41: find the errors and mark them in your book.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 2Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 11. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 3Read Adam of the Road.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 2Memorize and review for five minutes.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 22, Day: 2Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:38 AMPage 2 of 5

Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 233,identify the countries listed on p.169 and the items listed under ‘Map-It’ on p.170.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 3Memorize and review for five minutes.Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-1928 1-10) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 3Listen to the Arrival of the Queen of Sheba by Handel.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 22, Day: 3Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:38 AMPage 3 of 5

Also review any mistakes you made in the Adjective Review.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 4Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 11 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 5Finish Adam of the Road.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 4Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 22, Day: 4Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:38 AMPage 4 of 5

If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 1Write the best part of the story you just finished reading. If possible write this in the first person as if you were one of the charactersin the story. Just tell that part of the story as if you were telling it to a friend, but do it in writing. If this is too difficult, then you can writethe best part of the story in the third person as a narration.Assessment Assignment:Week 21, Day 1, Adam of the Road Retelling #1Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 5Optional: Do Art Pad #18, Night Sky Project, Part 2: Extreme View Perspective.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 22, Day: 5Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:38 AMPage 5 of 5

blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 8, Selection 40: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Begin Lesson 11. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read Adam of the Road.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work on the next 6 lines, up to "... with music." Review the previous lines.Review the poems you learned in Kindergarten (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of theselesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them.

The discussion will prepare you for the summaries in the next two days.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Write the best part of the story you just finished reading. If possible write this in the first person as if you were one of the charactersin the story. Just tell that part of the story as if you were telling it to a friend, but do it in writing. If this is too difficult, then you can writethe best part of the story in the third person as a narration.Assessment Assignment:Week 21, Day 1, Adam of the Road Retelling #1

parsed.raw_text:
Weekly List for John Paul McAdams
Monday, April 27, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 87
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the seven capital sins. Read Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do workbook pages 168-169. (T.E. pages 340-343.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 40: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Begin Lesson 11. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusWork on the next 6 lines, up to "... with music." Review the previous lines.Review the poems you learned in Kindergarten (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of theselesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Tuesday, April 28, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 88
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the four cardinal virtues. Discuss Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church using "Words to Know"and the discussion questions in Chapter 21 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). The exercisescould be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions in Chapter 21 ofthe activity book as a guide.) These can be done orally. The discussion will prepare you for the summaries in the next two days.
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 41: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 11. If there are any you do not know, or can't figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Wednesday, April 29, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Test 21
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the corporal works of mercy. Then write a short summary of the various kinds of law, whichconstitute the external guide of the moral law.Assessment Assignment:Week 20, Day 3, Summary of the Kinds of Law
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do Adjective Review on workbook pages 170-172. (T.E. pages 344-349.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 41: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Do the Lesson 11 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 11 Key Words you had trouble spelling.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Geography: Complete Chapter 5 (South America), Section 1 (The Continent) in Eighth Grade Geography.OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Read pp.169-170. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 233,identify the countries listed on p.169 and the items listed under ‘Map-It' on p.170.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-1928 1-10)Listen to the Arrival of the Queen of Sheba by Handel.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Thursday, April 30, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 89
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the spiritual works of mercy. Then write a short summary of the correct understanding ofconscience.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Today review anything in the adjective section that you had difficulty with. Also review any mistakes you made in the Adjective Review.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 11 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Finish Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Friday, May 1, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 90
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Take Adjective Test on page 23 in the Test Booklet. (T.E. pages 350-353.)
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Take a spelling test on Lesson 11 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Write the best part of the story you just finished reading. If possible write this in the first person as if you were one of the charactersin the story. Just tell that part of the story as if you were telling it to a friend, but do it in writing. If this is too difficult, then you can writethe best part of the story in the third person as a narration.Assessment Assignment:Week 21, Day 1, Adam of the Road Retelling #1
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Optional: Do Art Pad #18, Night Sky Project, Part 2: Extreme View Perspective.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for Joseph McAdamsMonday, April 27, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 2Lesson 74Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Read Chapter 23 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Eucharist/Mass and Papacy. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 1Do the Verb Review today, pages 105-107. (T.E. pages 200-205.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Lesson 7, Selection 36: find the errors and mark them in your book.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 1Begin Lesson 11. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 1Attend Class. Do the Lesson XXI Quia Games. History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 1Finish writing.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 4Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style.Music 7 (Alfred's Essentials of Music) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 5Listen to Handel's "Hallelujah" and "He was despised" from the "Messiah"4/27/26, 8:52 AMPage 1 of 7
 Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 1Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:52 AMPage 2 of 7
  
   
  Tuesday, April 28, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 3Lesson 75Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 2Discuss the questions for Chapter 23. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What device did Paul use to deflect interest from himself?2. How did Paul escape from the plot of the Jews?3. Where was Paul sent?4. Did the governor hear Paul’s case immediately?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 2Do the Verb Review today, pages 108-110. (T.E. pages 206-211.)Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 2Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 11. If there are any you do not know, or can’t figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 2Lesson 22. Review distinguishing between helping verbs and “to be” verbs (lesson XX). Read the twenty-second lesson. Oral Drill:decline the adjective “magnus, a, um” and the noun “dux, ducis.” Then put them together, remembering that “dux” is a masculinethird declension word. Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisdux, ducis, duci, ducem, duce, duces, ducum, ducibus, duces, ducibusmagnus dux, magni ducis, magno duci, magnum ducem, magno duce, magni duces, magnorumducum, magnis ducibus,magnos duces, magnis ducibusHistory & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Locate a good map of the Mediterranean Area. Trace (or draw freehand) the outline of Italy, including the Mediterranean region. Themaps on pp. 14-19 of Historical Atlas of the World by Rand McNally are all good maps for this. Photocopy it 6 times. Also, read TheOld World and America pp. 62-73.Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 2Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:52 AMPage 3 of 7
        Wednesday, April 29, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 4Lesson 76Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Read Chapter 24 of Acts. Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards youworked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 3Study for the verb test today. Review anything you are unsure of from the verb section of this book.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 3Do the Lesson 11 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 11 Key Words you had trouble spelling.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 3Lesson 22. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 3Read ‘Romulus’, ‘Numa Pompilius’, ‘Horatii’, and ‘The Tarquins’ in Famous Men of Rome, pp. 5-23 (pp. 1-22).Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 1Recite the whole passage each day this week. You may also write the section down in your poetry notebook, and even illustrate it.Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 3Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:52 AMPage 4 of 7
  
       Thursday, April 30, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 5Test 14Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 4Discuss the questions for Chapter 24, or write out your answers.1. Who came to accuse Paul? Of what did they accuse him?2. What was his defense?3. What did Felix do?4. Why do you think Felix was terrified by the things Paul preached?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 4Take the Verb Test on page 11 in the Test Booklet. (T.E. pages 212-221.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Lesson 7, Selection 36: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 4Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 11 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 4Lesson 22. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 4Read the chapter on Coriolanus in Lives from Plutarch: The Modern American Edition of Twelve. Read ‘Junius Brutus’, ‘Horatius’,‘Mucius’, ‘Coriolanus’, ‘The Fabii,’ and ‘Cincinnatus’ in Famous Men of Rome, pp. 25-45 (pp. 23-42). Note: You only need to readCoriolanus in one of the two texts.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 2Recite and also try to render the first four lines in prose. What is Portia saying?Music 7 (Alfred's Essentials of Music) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 3Listen to Handel's "Let the Bright Seraphim.4/27/26, 8:52 AMPage 5 of 7
 Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 20, Day: 4Read for about 1 hour.4/27/26, 8:52 AMPage 6 of 7
       Friday, May 1, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 1Lesson 77Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 2Lesson 7, Selection 37: find the errors and mark them in your book.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 21, Day: 5Take a spelling test on Lesson 11 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 24, Day: 5Lesson 22. blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style.

blocks.subject: Music 7
blocks.assignment_text: Listen to Handel's "Hallelujah" and "He was despised" from the "Messiah"

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 75

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 23. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What device did Paul use to deflect interest from himself?2. How did Paul escape from the plot of the Jews?3. Where was Paul sent?4. Did the governor hear Paul's case immediately?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Verb Review today, pages 108-110. (T.E. pages 206-211.)

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 11. If there are any you do not know, or can't figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Review distinguishing between helping verbs and "to be" verbs (lesson XX). Read the twenty-second lesson. Oral Drill:decline the adjective "magnus, a, um" and the noun "dux, ducis." Then put them together, remembering that "dux" is a masculinethird declension word. Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisdux, ducis, duci, ducem, duce, duces, ducum, ducibus, duces, ducibusmagnus dux, magni ducis, magno duci, magnum ducem, magno duce, magni duces, magnorumducum, magnis ducibus,magnos duces, magnis ducibus

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Locate a good map of the Mediterranean Area. Trace (or draw freehand) the outline of Italy, including the Mediterranean region. Themaps on pp. 14-19 of Historical Atlas of the World by Rand McNally are all good maps for this. Photocopy it 6 times. Also, read TheOld World and America pp. 62-73.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 76

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Chapter 24 of Acts. Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards youworked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Study for the verb test today. Review anything you are unsure of from the verb section of this book.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Lesson 11 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 11 Key Words you had trouble spelling.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read ‘Romulus', ‘Numa Pompilius', ‘Horatii', and ‘The Tarquins' in Famous Men of Rome, pp. 5-23 (pp. 1-22).

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Recite the whole passage each day this week. You may also write the section down in your poetry notebook, and even illustrate it.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Test 14

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 24, or write out your answers.1. Who came to accuse Paul? Of what did they accuse him?2. What was his defense?3. What did Felix do?4. Why do you think Felix was terrified by the things Paul preached?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take the Verb Test on page 11 in the Test Booklet. (T.E. pages 212-221.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 7, Selection 36: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 11 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read the chapter on Coriolanus in Lives from Plutarch: The Modern American Edition of Twelve. Read ‘Junius Brutus', ‘Horatius',‘Mucius', ‘Coriolanus', ‘The Fabii,' and ‘Cincinnatus' in Famous Men of Rome, pp. 25-45 (pp. 23-42). Note: You only need to readCoriolanus in one of the two texts.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Recite and also try to render the first four lines in prose. What is Portia saying?

blocks.subject: Music 7
blocks.assignment_text: Listen to Handel's "Let the Bright Seraphim.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 77

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 7, Selection 37: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take a spelling test on Lesson 11 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Take Quiz 22.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Recite and also try rendering the next 10 lines in prose.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Optional: Do Art Pad #18, Night Sky Project, Part 2: Extreme View Perspective.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for Joseph McAdams
Monday, April 27, 2026
Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 74
Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles)Read Chapter 23 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Eucharist/Mass and Papacy. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do the Verb Review today, pages 105-107. (T.E. pages 200-205.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 7, Selection 36: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Begin Lesson 11. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Attend Class. Do the Lesson XXI Quia Games. 
History & Geog 7 (Old World & America)Finish writing.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.
